examples.title: Algorithm: Insertion into a Binary Search Tree
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how to insert a new value into a binary search tree while maintaining the BST property.
examples.code:
ALGORITHM Insertion(root, value)
INPUT: root node of the BST, value to be inserted
OUTPUT: root node of the updated BST

BEGIN
 IF root is NULL THEN
 CREATE a new node with value
 RETURN new node
 END IF

 IF value < root.value THEN
 root.left = Insertion(root.left, value)
 ELSE IF value > root.value THEN
 root.right = Insertion(root.right, value)
 END IF

 RETURN root
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode defines a recursive algorithm for inserting a new value into a binary search tree. It checks if the current node is NULL, in which case it creates a new node with the given value. Otherwise, it compares the value to the current node's value and recursively calls itself on the left or right subtree accordingly.

examples.title: Python: Insertion into a Binary Search Tree
examples.description: Python implementation of the insertion algorithm for a binary search tree.
examples.explanation: This Python code defines a Node class to represent individual nodes in the binary search tree and an insert function to add new values to the tree while maintaining the BST property.

examples.title: C: Searching in a Binary Search Tree
examples.description: C implementation of the searching algorithm for a binary search tree.
examples.explanation: This C code defines a Node struct to represent individual nodes in the binary search tree and a search function to find a specific value in the tree.

examples.title: Java: Finding the Maximum Value in a Binary Search Tree
examples.description: Java implementation of the algorithm to find the maximum value in a binary search tree.
examples.explanation: This Java code defines a Node class to represent individual nodes in the binary search tree and a findMax function to find the maximum value in the tree by traversing to the rightmost node.
